etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: From "social" + "-able" from Latin "socialis" + "-able" (able to associate)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: Able associate; having friendly outgoing and sociable friendly with outgoing presence
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Able associate → friendly outgoing → contemporary sociable friendly and sociable outgoing with friendly presence
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Able associate → friendly outgoing → sociable friendly → outgoing understanding
